long_description:
Adapter Kind: 

Adapter Instance: VCDA - dirw002tor04mvcda.ca-tor.vmware.cloud.ibm.com - Collecting - Data receiving


Alert Description: VCDA Failed to connect to the vCenter Server Lookup Service

Alert ID: c72d7f6c-abeb-4579-9840-3ea5e4f47695

Alert Impact: health

Alert Name: VCDA Failed to connect to the vCenter Server Lookup Service

Alert Subtype: Availability

Alert Type: Application

Cancel Date: Unavailable

Control State: Open

Criticality: immediate

Host Name: vrops-data04.torpr.dir

Alert Link: https://172.25.2.55/vcf-operations/ui/inventory;mode=hierarchy;resourceId=47799b76-2579-4633-a2ac-2ebcd7925cc2;tab=alerts-and-symptoms;subTab=alerts;entityId=c72d7f6c-abeb-4579-9840-3ea5e4f47695

Notification Rule Description: 

Notification Rule Name: PD: VCDA Failed to connect to the vCenter Server Lookup Service

Object Efficiency State: 1.0

Object Health State: 3.0

Object Risk State: 2.0

Product: ['N/A', 'N/A', 'N/A']

Recommendations: Ensure that the associated vCenter Server Lookup Service is healthy and accessible from Cloud Director Availability. |
 Ensure that the clocks are synchronized for vCenter Server and all its associated components, like ESXi hosts, database server, and others. |
 Ensure that the vCenter Server Lookup Service URL used in Cloud Director Availability is reachable from the Cloud Director Replication Management Appliance. |
 Re-register the vCenter Server Lookup Service in the Cloud Director Availability Provider Portal.

Runbook URL: Runbook: https://pages.github.ibm.com/cloudlab/internal-docs/vrops_sev2_alerts.html

Resource ID: 47799b76-2579-4633-a2ac-2ebcd7925cc2

Resource Kind: VcdaSite

Resource Name: dirw002tor04mvcda

Status: Active

Symptoms: Symptom Set - self
Conditions

 System Health|Manager Service|Lookup Service connectivity != OKSymptom Set - self
Conditions
dirw002tor04mvcda | 47799b76-2579-4633-a2ac-2ebcd7925cc2 
 System Health|Cloud Service|Lookup Service connectivity != OK

Recommendations: Ensure that the associated vCenter Server Lookup Service is healthy and accessible from Cloud Director Availability. |
 Ensure that the clocks are synchronized for vCenter Server and all its associated components, like ESXi hosts, database server, and others. |
 Ensure that the vCenter Server Lookup Service URL used in Cloud Director Availability is reachable from the Cloud Director Replication Management Appliance. |
 Re-register the vCenter Server Lookup Service in the Cloud Director Availability Provider Portal.
